channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Chlorination-controlled aggregation and film-formation kinetics enabling high-efficiency organic solar cells with low-cost linear conjugated polymers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69051-6

Yin et al. report linear conjugated polymer donors with chlorinated backbone for modulating polymer aggregation and surface tension, and optimised compatibility with norfullerene acceptors. By using binary blends, a 20.42% efficiency is achieved for organic solar cells.
item.dc:title: Chlorination-controlled aggregation and film-formation kinetics enabling high-efficiency organic solar cells with low-cost linear conjugated polymers
item.dc:creator: Bingyan Yin
Zhili Chen
Baoqi Wu
Xiyue Yuan
Cong Xiao
Xia Zhou
Jianbin Zhong
Seunglok Lee
Wei Chen
Lifu Zhang
Liming Wang
Guangye Zhang
Wei Zhang
Changduk Yang
Zhitian Liu
Fei Huang
Yong Cao
Chunhui Duan

item.title: Deep learning drives autonomous molecular reactions with single-bond selectivity in tetra-brominated porphyrins on Au(111)
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69080-1

Controlling chemical reactions one bond at a time is a challenge in nanoscience. Here, the authors present an AI-controlled STM system that autonomously performs multistep, bond-selective reactions on individual molecules with high precision and reproducibility.
item.dc:title: Deep learning drives autonomous molecular reactions with single-bond selectivity in tetra-brominated porphyrins on Au(111)
item.dc:creator: Zhiwen Zhu
Qi Huang
Tairan Yang
Hao Jiang
Shaoxuan Yuan
Juan Xiang
Liangliang Cai
Qiang Sun
